message:
feat(runtime): reclaim region heap allocations on quiescence

Implements automatic memory reclamation for region-owned heap
allocations when a region transitions to the Closed state.

Changes to region.rs:
- Acquires write lock (was read lock) during Finalizing→Closed transition
- Calls inner.heap.reclaim_all() to release all region-owned allocations
- Ensures heap memory is returned to the allocator promptly

This change is important for long-running runtimes where regions are
created and closed frequently. Without explicit reclamation, heap
memory would remain allocated until the region record itself is dropped,
potentially causing memory pressure.

New test coverage:
- region_heap_reclaimed_on_close: Verifies heap_len() drops to 0 after
  complete_close() is called
- rref_invalid_after_close: Confirms that RRef handles correctly return
  RRefError::AllocationInvalid when accessed after region closure

The heap reclamation is triggered by complete_close() rather than
begin_close() to ensure all tasks have finished accessing heap data
before it is reclaimed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
